title: Tensorized Graph Neural Networks for Multi-Relational Knowledge Graph Completion
abstract: We propose a tensorized graph neural network that leverages CP decomposition to efficiently model multi-relational knowledge graphs. Our approach reduces the parameter count by an order of magnitude while maintaining competitive accuracy on standard link prediction benchmarks.

title: Grassmannian Manifold Optimization for Neural Network Training
abstract: This paper introduces a novel optimization framework that constrains weight matrices to lie on the Grassmann manifold during neural network training. The resulting Grassmannian gradient descent algorithm demonstrates improved generalization on image classification tasks.

title: Low-Rank Tensor Methods for Scalable Knowledge Graph Embedding
abstract: We present a scalable knowledge graph embedding method based on Tucker decomposition of the entity-relation tensor. Our approach achieves linear time complexity with respect to the number of entities and outperforms existing methods on large-scale benchmarks including Wikidata and Freebase.

title: Manifold-Regularized Attention Mechanisms for Graph Transformers
abstract: We incorporate Riemannian manifold regularization into the attention computation of graph transformers. By constraining attention heads to the Grassmannian, we achieve more stable training and better performance on molecular property prediction tasks.

title: A Survey of Tensor Decomposition Techniques in Machine Learning
abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive overview of tensor decomposition methods including CP, Tucker, and tensor train decompositions. We discuss their applications in recommender systems, NLP, computer vision, and knowledge graph modeling, along with recent algorithmic advances.

title: Riemannian Optimization for Deep Learning: A Geometric Perspective
abstract: We provide a unified geometric framework for understanding optimization on matrix manifolds in deep learning. Our analysis covers Stiefel, Grassmann, and orthogonal manifolds, and introduces efficient retractions and vector transport operations for SGD variants.

title: Tensor Completion on Graph-Structured Data via Grassmannian Embeddings
abstract: We propose a tensor completion method that leverages Grassmannian embeddings to capture the geometric structure of graph-structured data. Our approach combines spectral graph theory with tensor factorization to handle missing entries in knowledge graph tensors.

title: Neural Tensor Networks with Adaptive Rank Selection for Information Retrieval
abstract: We introduce an adaptive rank selection mechanism for neural tensor networks applied to information retrieval tasks. The model automatically determines the optimal tensor rank for each query-document pair, achieving state-of-the-art results on MS MARCO and TREC benchmarks.

title: Dense Passage Retrieval with Manifold-Aware Semantic Matching
abstract: We present a dense passage retrieval model that incorporates manifold-awareness into its semantic matching component. By projecting passage embeddings onto a learned Riemannian manifold, our model better captures the hierarchical structure of document collections.

title: Hierarchical Tensor Graph Networks for Multi-Scale Molecular Property Prediction
abstract: We propose a hierarchical tensor graph network that operates at multiple scales of molecular representation. The model uses tensor contraction to aggregate information across atomic, bond, and ring-level subgraphs, achieving improved accuracy on quantum chemistry benchmarks.
